summary: Clean, minimal implementation of transcript_tail that correctly bounds the goal-evaluator judge's per-turn payload. All required scope items present, edge cases handled, tests thorough.

issues.description: The optional integration test `goal_evaluator_receives_tail_only` was skipped. This would have directly verified the call-site change at line 877 (the acceptance criterion 'run_goal_loop passes a tail slice of the transcript to GoalEvaluator::evaluate') rather than just trusting the code reading. The goal spec marked it as optional, so this is not a blocker.
issues.suggestion: Consider adding a test that starts a goal loop and asserts the message count passed to the judge is bounded (e.g. by using a MockProvider that captures the messages or inspecting GoalEvaluator inputs). The existing run_goal_loop_* tests in the same file would be the right place to add it.

summary: Clean, minimal implementation that correctly bounds the goal-evaluator judge's per-turn payload via a new transcript_tail accessor; all required scope items are present, edge cases are handled, and tests thoroughly cover the new accessor.

issues.description: The optional integration test `goal_evaluator_receives_tail_only` (verifying the goal-loop call site at line 877 actually passes a bounded slice to the judge, not the full transcript) was skipped along with the optional env-var override. The goal spec explicitly marked this test as conditional on step 3, so it is not a blocker, but it is the only thing that exercises the acceptance criterion 'run_goal_loop passes a tail slice… to GoalEvaluator::evaluate' end-to-end rather than trusting a code-read. The current three tests cover the accessor in isolation.
issues.suggestion: If the existing `run_goal_loop_*` tests in the same file are amenable, add one that wires up a `MockProvider` whose captured message list is asserted to be bounded (≤ GOAL_EVAL_TRANSCRIPT_TAIL after several turns). Even a one-liner asserting `evaluator_input.len() <= 12` would harden the acceptance criterion against future drift. Not required for approval.

issues.description: The const doc comment is noticeably more verbose than the goal's suggested wording and mentions 'The judge still does its own internal TAIL slicing inside GoalEvaluator::evaluate' — the existence of an internal judge-side TAIL is not directly verified in this diff, so the comment is asserting behavior the reader cannot see in `src/runtime.rs`. Minor risk of being a lie if GoalEvaluator's internal logic changes.
issues.suggestion: Either drop the parenthetical claim about the judge's internal slicing (since this diff does not author that behavior) or replace it with a back-reference like 'See also `src/runtime_goal.rs` for the evaluator's internal windowing.' This keeps the comment authoritative.
